title: First rocket launch!
description: Print and launch your own model rocket. Follow the steps to print, assemble, and launch the rocket. Get ready for liftoff!

dialogue.text: Nova here—checklist first, fireworks second. Ready for your first model rocket launch?

options.text: You betcha! How, uh, how high is this rocket thing launching?

dialogue.text: I've flown this exact build: ~35 meters in perfect conditions. Wind can cut or stretch that.

options.text: Oh, cool, that doesn't sound very high, but I'm sure I'm underestimating what that looks like! And I don't need some special paperwork from the government to launch this thing?

dialogue.text: You're good. FAA hobby limits: under 1500 g total and under 125 g propellant. Our body is ~90 g and the motor ~18 g. Find an open field and we're set.

options.text: Gotcha, that quiets my fears a bit. So, what do I need to do to get this thing ready to launch?

dialogue.text: First we print the rocket. Lightweight PLA, about an hour if the printer behaves.

options.text: Print the rocket?? Aren't there, like, pre-made rockets I can buy?

dialogue.text: Store-bought works, but printing lets us iterate. It's four parts, then glue and assembly. We're building an MVP now and refining later.

options.text: Works for me! What do I need to print?

dialogue.text: Modular keeps failures small and revisions easy. Print four parts: nosecone, body tube, fin can, and nosecone coupler. One printer means one-at-a-time; batch it your way.

options.text: Fin! Well, not yet, but soon!
A couple of these should do the trick! Get it? A couple? Nosecone coupler? Hah! Actually I only need the one ...
Alright, all 4 components are now printed! What's next?

dialogue.text: Assembly time. You'll need a solid rocket motor, superglue, and Kevlar shock cord. Grab them, then follow the schematic and we'll lock it in.

options.text: The rocket's assembled! Are we ready for launch?

dialogue.text: Almost there. We still need a launch pad, igniter, and controller. Pad keeps it vertical, igniter fires the motor, controller triggers it. I have spares—use mine.

options.text: Oh cool, I'll never turn down free stuff!
We are go for launch!!! Right?

dialogue.text: We are go. Place the rocket on the pad, insert the igniter, and connect it to the controller. Step back to a safe distance and press launch.

options.text: Whoa! I can't believe I launched a rocket!! It didn't survive the landing, though, unfortunately.

dialogue.text: That's still a huge success. Welcome to rocketry. We'll iterate, recover what we can, and improve the next design. For now, enjoy the glow of liftoff.

options.text: Houston, we do not have a problem!
